title: Full Node vs Lightweight Wallet
description: Full nodes verify Bitcoin rules locally, while lightweight wallets trade some verification guarantees for lower bandwidth, storage, and setup costs. Choosing between them means choosing a trust model, not just a software footprint.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Operating Modes
resources.notes: Canonical overview of full-node validation, SPV tradeoffs, and the operating-mode choices available to wallet users.

resources.title: Bitcoin Dev Philosophy: Don’t trust, verify
resources.notes: User-facing explanation of why self-verification changes what guarantees a wallet can give you.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for wallet operating modes, full-node validation, and lightweight client tradeoffs.
